mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor demand that triggers risk-off positioning, depressing S&P 500 prices over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE jumps >0.3pp, or the signal shows no predictive power after controlling for concurrent FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous contraction in real GDP growth and a decline in consumer credit outstanding (RSAFS) indicates a synchronized demand shock that compresses corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive when both GDPC1 growth and RSAFS decline simultaneously, or the joint signal fails to outperform a single-proxy baseline in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: In an environment where industrial production exceeds its 12-month moving average while CPI remains below 2.5%, supply-side efficiency gains translate directly into higher profit margins, boosting S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO outpaces its trend while CPI stays below 2.5%, or the signal loses predictive power during periods of supply chain disruption.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in housing starts reflects rising business and consumer confidence, which historically precedes positive equity momentum over the subsequent week as capital expenditure plans accelerate.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero following housing starts increases, or the relationship reverses during credit crunch episodes.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, the cost of capital exceeds the historical equilibrium for corporate debt servicing, forcing margin compression and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive when FEDFUNDS sustains levels above 4.5% for consecutive quarters, or the threshold signal fails to predict drawdowns in high-leverage sectors.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where payroll employment grows while the unemployment rate simultaneously rises indicates a large labor force participation surge that dilutes wage pressure, signaling weak underlying demand and depressing equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive when PAYEMS and UNRATE move in this divergent pattern, or the interaction term shows no incremental explanatory power over a single-proxy model.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of rapid GDP growth acceleration, a concurrent spike in industrial production amplifies capacity utilization, driving short-term equity rallies as earnings revisions turn sharply positive over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when both GDP growth and industrial production accelerate simultaneously, or the signal fails to capture short-term momentum shifts in high-volatility regimes.
